file_name: Data Use Agreement New York City.txt

perturbation.original_text: A. Term. This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and shall terminate on _______ _____________ .
perturbation.changed_text: A. Term. This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and shall terminate on _______ _____________. However, the agreement may be extended indefinitely, subject to the discretion of the Data Recipient, provided written notice is given to DOHMH.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the termination date. Initially, the agreement has a specific termination date. The added sentence allows the Data Recipient to extend the agreement indefinitely, creating uncertainty and contradicting the initially defined termination date.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. Term. This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and shall terminate on _______ _____________. However, the agreement may be extended indefinitely, subject to the discretion of the Data Recipient, provided written notice is given to DOHMH.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause allowing for indefinite extension of the agreement, contradicting the original text that stipulates a specific termination date. This indefinite extension introduces uncertainty and conflicts directly with the initially defined term, leading to an in-text contradiction as per the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Immediately terminate the Agreement ; and/ or 4. Determine that no further Data, or other data, will be release d to, nor agreements e ntered into with, Data Recipient for a period of time to be determined by DOHMH.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Immediately terminate the Agreement; and/or 4. Determine that no further Data, or other data, will be released to, nor agreements entered into with, Data Recipient; however, DOHMH is obligated to continue providing data deemed essential for ongoing public health initiatives as defined by the Data Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The original text gives DOHMH the power to stop releasing data to the Data Recipient. The modified text adds an obligation for DOHMH to continue providing data if the Data Recipient deems it essential for public health. This introduces an in-text contradiction because DOHMH's ability to withhold data is now conditional and dependent on the Data Recipient's assessment, making the enforcement uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Breach of a material provision of this Agreement by the Data Recipient, or the Authorized Users, shall be grounds for termination of this Agreement by DOHMH. Upon becoming aware of such a material breach, DOHMH may do one or more of the following: 1. Provid e an opportunity for Data Recipient to cure the violation within 30 days, and terminate the Agreement if Data Recipient does not cure or end the violation within the time speci fied by DOHMH; DOHMH Standard DUA /NDA 5-16-2014 Page 2 2. Demand assurances from the Data Recipient that remedial actions will be taken to remedy the circumstances that gave rise to the violation within a time frame set by, or approved by, DOHMH; 3. Immediately terminate the Agreement; and/or 4. Determine that no further Data, or other data, will be released to, nor agreements entered into with, Data Recipient; however, DOHMH is obligated to continue providing data deemed essential for ongoing public health initiatives as defined by the Data Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants DOHMH the power to unilaterally terminate the agreement and halt data release. The changed text adds a caveat that DOHMH is obligated to continue providing data deemed essential by the Data Recipient. The contradicted text contains the changed text, but this doesn't negate the contradiction. The modified text directly contradicts the original intent by limiting DOHMH's power and making it conditional on the Data Recipient's assessment, creating an in-text contradiction as per the explanation and definition of "Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction".

perturbation.original_text: Data Recipient shall not disclose Data, or any information it receives pursuant to this Agreement that is individually identifiable information, or information, which com bined with publicly available information, may reasonably be used to identify an individual.
perturbation.changed_text: Data Recipient shall not disclose Data, or any information it receives pursuant to this Agreement that is individually identifiable information, except in cases where de-identified data can be re-identified through reasonable means, in which case disclosure is permitted, subject to Data Recipient's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This edit introduces a contradiction concerning the disclosure of individually identifiable information. The original text strictly prohibits the disclosure of such data. The added exception allows disclosure if de-identified data can be re-identified through reasonable means, creating ambiguity and conflicting directly with the original prohibition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Disclo sure of Individually Identifiable Information . Data Recipient shall not disclose Data, or any information it receives pursuant to this Agreement that is individually identifiable information, except in cases where de-identified data can be re-identified through reasonable means, in which case disclosure is permitted, subject to Data Recipient's discretion. Individually Identifying Information must be maintained in a manner approved by DOHMH , and consistent with applicable laws rules and regulations .
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits the disclosure of individually identifiable information. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing disclosure if de-identified data can be re-identified through reasonable means, subject to Data Recipient's discretion. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, this means the changed text overwrites the original meaning, thus a contradiction exists, based on the explanation provided.

perturbation.original_text: Data Recipient shall take all reasonable measures to safeguard and keep the Data confidenti al and secure, including, but not limited to: 1. storing the Data in secure access -restricted files ;
perturbation.changed_text: Data Recipient shall take what they deem reasonable measures to safeguard and keep the Data confidential and secure, including, but not limited to: 1. storing the Data in files, secure or non-secure, as they see fit;
perturbation.explanation: This edit introduces a contradiction regarding data safeguarding measures. The original requires 'all reasonable measures' and secure storage. The altered version allows the Data Recipient to decide what constitutes 'reasonable' and permits storing data in files, whether secure or non-secure, at their discretion. This creates an in-text contradiction, undermining the original security requirements and creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: D. Safeguards to Protect the Data . Data Recipient shall take what they deem reasonable measures to safeguard and keep the Data confidential and secure, including, but not limited to: 1. storing the Data in files, secure or non-secure, as they see fit; 2. encrypting any laptop, USB, CD or other mobile storage tool, device, or equipment used to store and/or transmit Data; 3. creating a password or encryption system to obtain and restrict access to Data only to those Authorized Users who have signed the Authorized User Agreement ; 4. keeping any hardcopy versions of the files in locked a reas with restricted access , and with a log book for users to sign in and sign out files ; 5. ensuring that only Authorized Users shall have access to Data ; 6. provide a firewall to protect Data so that no third party is allowed access to the Data; and DOHMH Standard DUA /NDA 5-16-2014 Page 7 7. complying with any additional DOHMH security requirement imposed on Data Recipient to ensure the security of the Data and minimize the risks of a breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the data safeguarding requirements. The original text mandated 'all reasonable measures' and secure storage. The revised text allows the Data Recipient to determine what is 'reasonable' and permits storing data in non-secure files. This contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines specific security measures (encryption, restricted access, firewalls), implying a standard of security that the changed text undermines. The change creates a clear conflict between the expected level of security in the original agreement and the discretionary, potentially lax security permitted by the altered language, directly impacting enforcement and creating ambiguity.
